name: Apple Inc.
shortDescription: iPhone maker. The world's most valuable consumer hardware company, increasingly a services + AI play too.

name: Microsoft Corporation
shortDescription: Cloud (Azure), enterprise software, AI partner of OpenAI. One of the biggest beneficiaries of the AI build-out.

name: Alphabet Inc. (Class A)
shortDescription: Google Search, YouTube, Android, Google Cloud, Gemini AI, Waymo (self-driving). Cash machine + AI giant.

name: Amazon.com Inc.
shortDescription: Largest online retailer + AWS (the biggest cloud provider). Two very different businesses in one.

name: Meta Platforms Inc.
shortDescription: Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, Threads. Heavy spender on AI and VR. Ad-driven revenue.

name: Netflix Inc.
shortDescription: Streaming pioneer. Now with ads, gaming, and live sports as growth bets.

name: Oracle Corporation
shortDescription: Enterprise databases pivoting to cloud (OCI). Major AI-infrastructure partner for OpenAI and others.

name: NVIDIA Corporation
shortDescription: The chip maker powering the AI boom. Their GPUs are the picks-and-shovels of the AI build-out.

name: Advanced Micro Devices Inc.
shortDescription: CPU and GPU maker — the main competitor to Intel and Nvidia. Trying to catch up in AI chips.

name: Palantir Technologies Inc.
shortDescription: Defence and enterprise AI/data analytics. Used by US military, intelligence agencies, and large corporations.

name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.
shortDescription: The world's biggest chip foundry. Makes the most advanced chips for Apple, Nvidia, AMD and others.

name: ASML Holding N.V.
shortDescription: Dutch monopoly on EUV lithography machines — the equipment needed to make the most advanced chips.

name: Broadcom Inc.
shortDescription: Designs custom chips for hyperscalers (Google, Meta) and networking gear. Major AI infrastructure player.

name: Qualcomm Incorporated
shortDescription: Designs Snapdragon mobile chips and modems. Most smartphones outside Apple use their tech.

name: Micron Technology Inc.
shortDescription: Memory chips (DRAM, NAND, HBM). Cyclical but the AI HBM boom has been transformative.

name: Intel Corporation
shortDescription: The legacy x86 leader. Trying a multi-year turnaround focused on foundry services and PC chips.

name: Salesforce Inc.
shortDescription: The biggest CRM software company. Pushing hard into AI agents for sales and customer support.

name: Adobe Inc.
shortDescription: Creative software (Photoshop, Premiere). Trying to defend its turf against AI image/video generators.

name: ServiceNow Inc.
shortDescription: Enterprise workflow automation platform. Strong AI agent narrative for IT and HR.

name: Snowflake Inc.
shortDescription: Cloud data warehouse. The 'data layer' a lot of enterprise AI workloads run on.

name: Datadog Inc.
shortDescription: Observability platform — monitors cloud apps. Benefits when cloud spending grows.

name: CrowdStrike Holdings Inc.
shortDescription: Cybersecurity platform (endpoint protection). Premium valuation but consistent growth.

name: MongoDB Inc.
shortDescription: Document database platform — popular alternative to traditional SQL databases.

name: Tesla Inc.
shortDescription: EV maker turned AI / robotaxi / robot / energy company depending on how you squint. Wildly volatile.

name: Ford Motor Company
shortDescription: Classic Detroit automaker. Truck-heavy, struggling with EV transition costs.

name: General Motors Company
shortDescription: Largest US automaker by sales. Slow EV pivot but profitable ICE business.

name: Stellantis N.V.
shortDescription: Owns Jeep, Chrysler, Peugeot, Fiat, Maserati. European-American combo, currently under pressure.

name: Rivian Automotive Inc.
shortDescription: US electric truck/SUV startup. Loss-making but with Amazon as a big customer.

name: Toyota Motor Corporation
shortDescription: World's biggest car company by volume. Cautious on full EVs, leans hybrid — has been a winning strategy lately.

name: BYD Company Limited
shortDescription: Chinese EV + battery giant. Outsells Tesla in many markets. Hugely vertically integrated.

name: Mercedes-Benz Group AG
shortDescription: German luxury automaker. Premium pricing but slower in EV transition than Tesla/BYD.

name: Lockheed Martin Corporation
shortDescription: F-35 fighter jet maker, missiles, helicopters. The biggest US defence contractor.

name: RTX Corporation
shortDescription: Missiles, radars, jet engines (Pratt & Whitney). Diversified defence + commercial aerospace.

name: Northrop Grumman Corporation
shortDescription: Stealth bombers (B-21), nuclear missiles, satellites. Heavy on classified contracts.

name: General Dynamics Corporation
shortDescription: Tanks (Abrams), submarines, business jets (Gulfstream). Mix of defence + private aviation.

name: The Boeing Company
shortDescription: Commercial planes + defence. Multi-year struggle with 737 MAX safety issues and labour disputes.

name: Rheinmetall AG
shortDescription: German defence champion. Tanks, artillery, ammunition. Massive beneficiary of European rearmament.

name: Exxon Mobil Corporation
shortDescription: Largest US oil major. Big buybacks, leaning into LNG and chemicals.

name: Chevron Corporation
shortDescription: Second-largest US oil major. Dividend-focused.

name: Shell plc
shortDescription: UK-headquartered oil major. Cash-generative but climate transition is a slow-burn issue.

name: BP p.l.c.
shortDescription: British oil major. Recently scaled back its renewable energy push to focus back on hydrocarbons.

name: TotalEnergies SE
shortDescription: French oil major with a more substantial renewable energy bet than peers.

name: Enphase Energy Inc.
shortDescription: Solar microinverters + home batteries. Hit hard by high interest rates; recovery still uncertain.

name: First Solar Inc.
shortDescription: US-based utility-scale solar panel maker. Benefits from Inflation Reduction Act subsidies.

name: NextEra Energy Inc.
shortDescription: Largest US renewable energy producer + Florida utility. Steady dividend grower.

name: Plug Power Inc.
shortDescription: Hydrogen fuel cells. Highly speculative — has consistently lost money but still a hydrogen pure-play.

name: Booking Holdings Inc.
shortDescription: Owns Booking.com, Priceline, Kayak. Cash-generative online travel agency.

name: Airbnb Inc.
shortDescription: Global short-term rentals marketplace. Strong brand, regulatory headwinds in major cities.

name: Marriott International Inc.
shortDescription: World's largest hotel chain (Sheraton, Westin, St. Regis, Ritz-Carlton…). Asset-light franchise model.

name: Delta Air Lines Inc.
shortDescription: Big US airline. Premium-focused strategy is paying off.

name: Intuitive Surgical Inc.
shortDescription: Makers of the da Vinci surgical robot system. Effectively a monopoly in robotic surgery.

name: ABB Ltd
shortDescription: Swiss industrial automation + robotics + electrification. Big in factory automation.

name: IonQ Inc.
shortDescription: Trapped-ion quantum computing pure-play. Highly speculative, not profitable. Big swings.

name: Rigetti Computing Inc.
shortDescription: Superconducting quantum computing. Tiny revenue, big narrative. Trade with caution.

name: D-Wave Quantum Inc.
shortDescription: Quantum annealing (different approach from gate-based). Niche use cases, very speculative.

name: Eli Lilly and Company
shortDescription: Maker of Mounjaro/Zepbound (GLP-1 weight loss drugs). Generational growth story unfolding.

name: Novo Nordisk A/S
shortDescription: Danish maker of Ozempic/Wegovy — the original GLP-1 weight-loss drugs. Worldwide demand.

name: Johnson & Johnson
shortDescription: Diversified pharma/medical devices giant. Slow steady grower with a strong dividend.

name: AstraZeneca PLC
shortDescription: UK/Swedish pharma giant. Strong oncology pipeline.

name: AbbVie Inc.
shortDescription: Maker of Humira (long the world's best-selling drug) plus Skyrizi and Botox. Strong cash flows.

name: LVMH Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton
shortDescription: Louis Vuitton, Dior, Tiffany, Sephora, Moët, Hennessy… The luxury empire. Sensitive to Chinese spending.

name: Hermès International
shortDescription: Birkin and Kelly bags. The ultra-premium of the luxury world — outperforms because their pricing power is unique.

name: Compagnie Financière Richemont SA
shortDescription: Cartier, Van Cleef & Arpels, IWC, Piaget. Swiss watches + jewellery focused.

name: Ferrari N.V.
shortDescription: The supercar maker. Tiny production volumes + insane pricing power = a luxury business not really a carmaker.

name: MercadoLibre Inc.
shortDescription: The 'Amazon of Latin America' — e-commerce + payments (Mercado Pago). Strong growth in Brazil/Argentina/Mexico.

name: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd
shortDescription: Chinese e-commerce giant (Taobao, Tmall) + cloud + Ant Group. Hammered by regulatory crackdowns 2021-23.

name: JD.com Inc.
shortDescription: Chinese e-commerce with a focus on logistics and authentic-goods supply chain. Alibaba competitor.

name: JPMorgan Chase & Co.
shortDescription: Largest US bank. Diversified — consumer banking, investment banking, asset management.

name: Bank of America Corporation
shortDescription: Big US consumer + commercial bank. Closely tied to US economic cycle.

name: The Goldman Sachs Group Inc.
shortDescription: Top investment bank — trading, M&A advisory, asset management. Cyclical earnings.

name: HSBC Holdings plc
shortDescription: Global bank with heavy Asia exposure (HK, China, Singapore). Pivoting back toward Asia.

name: DBS Group Holdings Ltd
shortDescription: Singapore's biggest bank. Consistent dividend payer, well-managed, regional Asia exposure.

name: Singapore Airlines Ltd
shortDescription: Premium long-haul carrier. Cyclical but Singapore's flag carrier with iconic service.

name: Singapore Telecommunications Ltd
shortDescription: Singapore telecom incumbent + stakes in regional telcos (Optus, Airtel). Dividend-focused.

name: Sea Limited
shortDescription: Singapore-headquartered: Shopee (e-commerce in SEA), Garena (gaming), SeaMoney (fintech). High growth, volatile.

name: Uber Technologies Inc.
shortDescription: Ride-hailing + food delivery (Uber Eats) + freight. Finally consistently profitable after years of cash burn.

name: Spotify Technology S.A.
shortDescription: Music streaming leader globally. Heavy investment in podcasts and audiobooks. Margins are slowly improving.

name: Cloudflare Inc.
shortDescription: Internet infrastructure: CDN, DDoS protection, edge compute. Quietly becoming an AI inference platform.

name: Shopify Inc.
shortDescription: E-commerce platform for small/medium merchants. The main alternative to selling on Amazon.

name: Dell Technologies Inc.
shortDescription: PC maker, but the AI-server business (selling Nvidia-powered servers) has been the growth driver lately.

name: Roblox Corporation
shortDescription: User-generated games platform mainly for kids/teens. Often pitched as a metaverse play.

name: The Walt Disney Company
shortDescription: Theme parks, Disney+ streaming, ESPN, movies (Marvel, Pixar, Star Wars). Multi-year transition challenge.

name: Roku Inc.
shortDescription: Streaming-TV OS + ad platform. Cyclical with advertising spend.

name: Arista Networks Inc.
shortDescription: High-end data-centre switches. Major beneficiary of the AI build-out — Meta and Microsoft are huge customers.

name: Arm Holdings plc
shortDescription: UK chip designer. Owns the architecture used in nearly every smartphone. Pushing into AI/data-centre CPUs.

name: Marvell Technology Inc.
shortDescription: Custom chips for hyperscalers + storage/networking silicon. Big AI infrastructure exposure.

name: Lam Research Corporation
shortDescription: Wafer-fabrication equipment. Sells to TSMC, Samsung, Intel. Cyclical with chip-fab capex.

name: KLA Corporation
shortDescription: Inspection/metrology equipment for chip fabs. Less talked-about than ASML but similarly entrenched.

name: Applied Materials Inc.
shortDescription: Largest US chip-equipment maker. Sells deposition and etching gear to every major foundry.

name: Atlassian Corporation
shortDescription: Jira, Confluence, Bitbucket — developer productivity tools. Most software teams use at least one of their products.

name: HubSpot Inc.
shortDescription: Marketing + sales CRM software for SMBs. Less enterprise-y than Salesforce.

name: Zscaler Inc.
shortDescription: Cloud-based security (SASE/zero-trust). Premium-priced but hot in enterprise security.

name: Okta Inc.
shortDescription: Identity-as-a-service — single sign-on for the enterprise. Has had high-profile breaches.

name: Workday Inc.
shortDescription: Cloud HR and finance software for large enterprises. Steady enterprise grower.

name: IBM Corporation
shortDescription: Legacy enterprise tech now focused on hybrid cloud (Red Hat) and consulting. Decent dividend.

name: NIO Inc.
shortDescription: Chinese EV maker. Premium positioning, battery-swap network. Loss-making, competitive Chinese EV market.

name: XPeng Inc.
shortDescription: Chinese EV maker with a focus on autonomous-driving tech. Competing in a brutal Chinese price war.

name: Li Auto Inc.
shortDescription: Chinese EV maker focused on range-extender SUVs for families. Profitable, unusual for a new EV brand.

name: Huntington Ingalls Industries Inc.
shortDescription: Largest US shipbuilder. Builds Navy aircraft carriers and submarines. Hard to disrupt — gov contracts.

name: Occidental Petroleum Corporation
shortDescription: US oil + chemicals. Famously a Warren Buffett favourite. Big carbon-capture ambitions.

name: EOG Resources Inc.
shortDescription: US shale oil/gas producer. Lower-cost than peers, disciplined capital returns.

name: Equinor ASA
shortDescription: Norwegian state-controlled energy giant. Oil/gas + meaningful renewables push (offshore wind).

name: Sunrun Inc.
shortDescription: Largest US residential solar installer. Subscription model — like a utility but with rooftop solar.

name: SolarEdge Technologies Inc.
shortDescription: Solar inverters + storage. Israeli-headquartered. Hit hard by the 2022-23 solar downturn.

name: Hilton Worldwide Holdings Inc.
shortDescription: Hilton, Conrad, Waldorf Astoria, Hampton Inn… Franchise model — lower capital intensity than owning hotels.

name: Royal Caribbean Cruises Ltd
shortDescription: Largest cruise operator. Strong rebound post-COVID. High debt load is a feature of the cruise business.

name: Carnival Corporation
shortDescription: Biggest cruise company by ship count (Carnival, Princess, Holland America, Cunard). Heavily indebted.

name: Rockwell Automation Inc.
shortDescription: US industrial automation leader. Sells factory-floor control systems and software.

name: Pfizer Inc.
shortDescription: US pharma giant. Post-COVID slump — they're hunting for the next blockbuster franchise.

name: Merck & Co. Inc.
shortDescription: Maker of Keytruda — the world's best-selling cancer drug. Looking ahead to its patent cliff.

name: Novartis AG
shortDescription: Swiss pharma giant. Strong oncology pipeline, recently spun off Sandoz (generics).

name: Bristol-Myers Squibb Company
shortDescription: Big US pharma. Eliquis (blood thinner) is the cash cow but faces a patent cliff this decade.

name: Gilead Sciences Inc.
shortDescription: HIV + hepatitis-C leader. Newer cancer franchise is the growth bet.

name: The Estée Lauder Companies Inc.
shortDescription: Premium cosmetics (Estée Lauder, La Mer, MAC, Clinique). Heavy China exposure — bruised the last 2-3 years.

name: Kering S.A.
shortDescription: Owns Gucci, Saint Laurent, Bottega Veneta, Balenciaga. Currently struggling vs LVMH and Hermès.

name: Etsy Inc.
shortDescription: Marketplace for handmade/vintage goods. Struggled to grow since pandemic peak.

name: PayPal Holdings Inc.
shortDescription: Digital payments + Venmo. Was a darling, now a turnaround story.

name: Visa Inc.
shortDescription: The biggest card network. Toll-collector on global card spending. Wide moat, predictable cash flows.

name: Mastercard Incorporated
shortDescription: The other big card network. Same toll-collector model as Visa. Often moves in lockstep.

name: BlackRock Inc.
shortDescription: World's largest asset manager — runs ~$10 trillion. Owner of iShares (the ETF brand).

name: American Express Company
shortDescription: Premium card network + bank. Charges merchant fees + cardholder fees. Buffett favourite.

name: Morgan Stanley
shortDescription: Investment bank + large wealth management business (E*TRADE).

name: The Charles Schwab Corporation
shortDescription: US brokerage giant. Owns TD Ameritrade. Earnings sensitive to interest rates.

name: United Overseas Bank Limited
shortDescription: Singapore's third-largest bank. Strong ASEAN presence. Solid dividend payer.

name: Oversea-Chinese Banking Corporation Limited
shortDescription: Singapore's second-largest bank. Major wealth-management focus (Bank of Singapore subsidiary).

name: Wilmar International Limited
shortDescription: Asian agribusiness giant — palm oil, sugar, rice. Listed in Singapore, big China exposure.

name: CapitaLand Investment Limited
shortDescription: Singapore-based real estate manager. Asia-Pacific malls, offices, lodging. Asset-light spin-off of CapitaLand.

name: Singapore Technologies Engineering Ltd
shortDescription: Singapore's defence + aerospace + smart-city engineering champion. State-linked.

name: Jardine Matheson Holdings Limited
shortDescription: Hong Kong-based conglomerate listed in Singapore. Real estate, motor dealerships, supermarkets, financial services.

name: Walmart Inc.
shortDescription: World's largest retailer. Both physical stores and a fast-growing e-commerce business.

name: Costco Wholesale Corporation
shortDescription: Membership-based warehouse retailer. Earns most of its profit from membership fees, not margins on goods.

name: The Home Depot Inc.
shortDescription: US home-improvement leader. Cyclical with housing — has held up well through high interest rates.

name: NIKE Inc.
shortDescription: World's biggest sportswear brand. Struggling vs newer entrants (On, Hoka, Lululemon) and weak China.

name: Starbucks Corporation
shortDescription: World's biggest coffee chain. In turnaround mode after weak China + slow US transactions.

name: McDonald's Corporation
shortDescription: Iconic fast-food chain. Largely franchised — earns fees from operators. Steady dividend.

name: Lululemon Athletica Inc.
shortDescription: Premium athletic wear (yoga + men's). After years of growth, slowing now.

name: Verizon Communications Inc.
shortDescription: US wireless + fibre carrier. High dividend, slow grower. Big infrastructure capex.

name: T-Mobile US Inc.
shortDescription: US carrier benefiting from the Sprint merger synergies. Best 5G coverage. Growing subscribers fastest.

name: Vodafone Group plc
shortDescription: UK-headquartered global telecom. Pan-European operations, plus Africa. Long dividend history; recently cut.

name: Caterpillar Inc.
shortDescription: Yellow construction equipment + mining gear + engines. Strongly cyclical with construction spending.

name: Deere & Company
shortDescription: Agricultural equipment leader. Cyclical with farm income. Pushing into precision agriculture / AI in the field.

name: GE Aerospace
shortDescription: Post-spin-off, GE is purely jet engines (LEAP for Airbus/Boeing single-aisles). Air-travel cycle exposure.

name: Honeywell International Inc.
shortDescription: Diversified industrial — aerospace systems, building automation, safety, performance materials.

name: Coinbase Global Inc.
shortDescription: Largest US crypto exchange. Revenue swings wildly with crypto-market volume + Bitcoin price.

name: MicroStrategy Incorporated
shortDescription: Software company that pivoted to a 'Bitcoin treasury' strategy — holds billions in BTC. Effectively a leveraged Bitcoin proxy.

name: Airbus SE
shortDescription: Europe's aerospace giant and Boeing's main rival. Commercial jets (A320/A350), plus defence, helicopters and space.

name: Safran SA
shortDescription: Jet engines (co-makes the LEAP with GE) and aircraft equipment. Earns for decades servicing engines it sold.

name: Thales SA
shortDescription: French defence-electronics champion: radars, secure comms, avionics, cybersecurity. European rearmament play.

name: Schneider Electric SE
shortDescription: Global leader in electrical gear and energy management — a core electrification and data-centre-power play.

name: L'Air Liquide SA
shortDescription: Industrial gases (oxygen, nitrogen, hydrogen) for industry and healthcare. Steady, defensive compounder.

name: Vinci SA
shortDescription: Construction and concessions — builds and operates motorways and airports. Toll/airport cash flows + building.

name: L'Oréal SA
shortDescription: World's biggest cosmetics group (Lancôme, Maybelline, La Roche-Posay). Defensive consumer with premium tilt.

name: Pernod Ricard SA
shortDescription: Global spirits house (Absolut, Jameson, Martell, Chivas). Premium drinks brand portfolio.

name: Sanofi SA
shortDescription: French pharma major — immunology blockbuster Dupixent plus vaccines. Defensive healthcare.

name: Dassault Systèmes SE
shortDescription: 3D design and engineering software (CATIA, SolidWorks). High-margin industrial software compounder.

name: Capgemini SE
shortDescription: Global IT services and consulting — digital transformation, cloud, and AI integration for enterprises.

name: BNP Paribas SA
shortDescription: The eurozone's largest bank by assets. Retail, corporate and investment banking across Europe.

name: AXA SA
shortDescription: One of the world's biggest insurers — life, property & casualty, and asset management.

name: Accor SA
shortDescription: Europe's largest hotel group — brands from luxury (Sofitel, Raffles, Fairmont) to budget (Ibis). Travel-cycle exposure.

name: OVH Groupe SA
shortDescription: France's largest cloud and web-hosting provider — a European, 'sovereign' alternative to AWS and Azure.
